pi_items.source: task.json metadata and task description; verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which use WS_NAME='imagery'
task.json metadata and task description; verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which use STORE_NAME='world_mosaic_store'
task.json metadata and task description; verified in setup_task.sh and export_result.sh which use LAYER_NAME='global_mask'
setup_task.sh creates directory /opt/geoserver/data_dir/mosaics/world_quadrants; export_result.sh checks for 'mosaics/world_quadrants' in store URL
setup_task.sh generate_tile() function specifies exact GDAL rasterization parameters for all 4 quadrants

pi_items.verified_value: 4 tiles covering full global extent: NW(-180 0 0 90), NE(0 0 180 90), SW(-180 -90 0 0), SE(0 -90 180 0)
pi_items.source: setup_task.sh generate_tile() calls with geographic extents; global extent: -180 -90 180 90
setup_task.sh specifies -ot Byte in gdal_rasterize command
setup_task.sh specifies -burn 255 for land and -init 0 for water background in gdal_rasterize command
setup_task.sh specifies -a_srs EPSG:4326 in gdal_rasterize command
setup_task.sh specifies -co COMPRESS=LZW in gdal_rasterize command

privileged_info_summary: The task creates an ImageMosaic layer in GeoServer from 4 raster tiles covering the full global extent at 1.0x1.0 degree resolution. The tiles are generated dynamically from the Natural Earth ne_110m_admin_0_countries.shp dataset (version 5.1.1, public domain) using gdal_rasterize with EPSG:4326 coordinates, Byte data type, and LZW compression. Land areas are represented as 255 (white) and water as 0 (black). The expected workspace is 'imagery', the store is 'world_mosaic_store' (type ImageMosaic), the layer is 'global_mask', and the mosaic directory path is 'mosaics/world_quadrants' relative to GeoServer's data directory.
